articles.title: Bomb threat reportedly halts plane at Logan ‘for hours’ before being deemed ‘not credible’
articles.description:
An unidentified person called MassPort and claimed there was a bomb aboard a Boston Logan Airport Plane, State Police said.

The post Bomb threat reportedly halts plane at Logan ‘for hours’ before being deemed ‘not credible’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: MassPort received a bomb threat affecting a plane at Logan Airport on March 15.
State Police responded, leading to hours of delays for passengers and crew.
The threat was later deemed not credible, ensuring safety for Boston travelers.

articles.title: 17 arrested during St. Patrick’s Day parade in South Boston, police say
articles.description:
Dozens of "borgs," or gallons of mixed alcoholic drinks, were confiscated at Broadway in the first half-hour of the celebration.

The post 17 arrested during St. Patrick’s Day parade in South Boston, police say appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Seventeen individuals were arrested during the St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston.
Police confiscated dozens of 'borgs' within the first half-hour of the event.
Increased law enforcement presence aimed to ensure public safety for Boston residents.

articles.title: All the way from Indonesia, she’s running the Boston Marathon for people with visual impairments
articles.description:
"If my miles can help even one person regain their sight and return to their daily activities with hope, then every step is worth it."

The post All the way from Indonesia, she’s running the Boston Marathon for people with visual impairments  appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Fransisca Miraningtyas is running the Boston Marathon to support visually impaired individuals.
She aims to raise awareness and funds for sight restoration efforts.
Her participation highlights the need for accessible resources in Boston for the visually impaired.

articles.title: CEOs of top airlines demand Congress restore funding to Homeland Security and pay airport workers
articles.description:
“Once again, air travel is the political football amid another government shutdown,” the executives wrote in an open letter to Congress that was published Sunday online and in The Washington Post.

The post CEOs of top airlines demand Congress restore funding to Homeland Security and pay airport workers appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Airline CEOs urge Congress to restore Homeland Security funding amid potential government shutdown.
Executives from major airlines emphasize the need for airport worker pay restoration in their letter.
Boston travelers may face disruptions if funding issues are not resolved before the shutdown.

articles.title: 5 takeaways from the Revolution’s 6-1 dismantling of FC Cincinnati
articles.description:
New England tied a club record for goals in a single MLS regular season game.

The post 5 takeaways from the Revolution’s 6-1 dismantling of FC Cincinnati appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: New England Revolution scored 6 goals against FC Cincinnati on March 15, 2023.
This victory tied the club record for goals in a single MLS regular season game.
Local fans celebrate a significant win, boosting team morale and community pride.
